proofRules: Do not use main as target truth.
Final videos use human-demo speed; qa-fast is only a dry-run or stability probe.
Do not create internal proof, route, process or ticket labels as product UI.
Do not claim Process-Universe completion while the coverage matrix blocks completion claims.
Advisor approval is not compliance release.
Upload success is not evidence sufficiency.
Export preview is not approval, download or share.

journeys.name: New user setup to first scoped workspace

valueNarrative.userNeed: A new user needs a clear path from blocked access to the right tenant, role and first client workspace.
valueNarrative.confidenceMoment: The user sees both wrong credentials and wrong MFA fail before valid seeded authentication opens only scoped work.
valueNarrative.safetyBoundary: Access does not become broad platform power; tenant, team and client context stay separated.
valueNarrative.resultValue: The first workspace is usable without exposing clients, roles or objects outside the user's scope.

businessSteps: Open the login surface in a blocked starting state.
Enter an invalid user identity.
Submit the invalid sign-in attempt.
Observe the blocked authentication feedback.
Replace the identity with a seeded invited user.
Start the valid provider login.
Open the MFA challenge.
Enter an invalid MFA code.
Observe the MFA blocker.
Submit the valid seeded MFA code.
Load the tenant setup workspace.
Switch to a platform-admin tenant setup perspective.
Review team assignment context.
Switch to a principal client workspace perspective.
Open the first scoped client workspace and verify client-safe orientation.
journeys.proofClass: Human-speed visible auth, role, tenant and first-workspace proof with API-backed seeded JWT transitions.

captions.intro: A new user starts blocked, authenticates with seeded MFA, then lands in scoped tenant and client workspace context.

journeys.name: Client context, object selection and upload-not-sufficiency boundary

valueNarrative.userNeed: The client team needs to find the right client object, update context and understand what is still missing.
valueNarrative.confidenceMoment: Profile, family, entity and document context are visible as related but distinct work surfaces.
valueNarrative.safetyBoundary: A document or upload state never silently becomes evidence sufficiency, release or client delivery.
valueNarrative.resultValue: The team can keep progressing on context while the system prevents premature reliance on incomplete evidence.

businessSteps: Authenticate into the scoped principal context.
Open client home and confirm the Morgan work context.
Open the client profile surface.
Edit a profile field as object-context input.
Save the profile draft without release.
Open family-member object selection.
Review the permitted family object state.
Open entity context for related structures.
Open the document upload workspace.
Observe the blocked source-file requirement.
Prove client context cannot silently create evidence sufficiency.
Move to the document review queue.
Filter the review queue for the tax-residency document.
Open the analyst review surface.
Verify the denied mutation stayed fail-closed.
journeys.proofClass: Human-speed client context and document state proof; upload remains distinct from sufficiency and release.

captions.intro: The client context journey shows object selection and document state without pretending that upload is evidence sufficiency.

journeys.name: Evidence rejection, recovery and sufficiency review

valueNarrative.userNeed: An analyst needs to turn incomplete evidence into a clear recovery path instead of guessing or approving too early.
valueNarrative.confidenceMoment: Search, review, blocker and recovery views show why the work is not sufficient yet and who must act next.
valueNarrative.safetyBoundary: Rejected or missing evidence remains blocked until recovery is represented and reviewed.
valueNarrative.resultValue: The case moves from uncertainty to reviewable evidence with a visible record of the negative and recovery states.

businessSteps: Authenticate as the analyst reviewer.
Open the document review queue.
Filter for the Morgan tax-residency document.
Open the selected review item.
Observe the human review state.
Attempt the sufficiency path before recovery.
Record the blocker as an evidence request.
Open the action board for missing evidence.
Filter the action board for blocked work.
Open the selected action detail.
Request missing information.
Switch to a client-success recovery perspective.
Open the client-safe evidence summary.
Return to analyst evidence review.
Confirm the recovered evidence can be reviewed without client release overclaim.
journeys.proofClass: Visible recovery workflow plus API/runtime proof; negative and recovery states stay explicit.

captions.intro: The analyst rejects incomplete evidence, routes recovery work, and only then returns to sufficiency review.

journeys.name: Internal draft to advisor approval without compliance release

valueNarrative.userNeed: The advisor needs to improve or approve an internal recommendation without accidentally making it client-visible.
valueNarrative.confidenceMoment: The advisor can find the case, request changes, revise rationale and approve into the next gate.
valueNarrative.safetyBoundary: Advisor approval is explicitly not compliance release and not client visibility.
valueNarrative.resultValue: The recommendation becomes ready for compliance review while the client remains protected from unapproved advice.

businessSteps: Open the analyst advisory hub.
Move into the advisory review queue.
Open the internal draft review work.
Verify the draft is still internal.
Switch to the senior advisor perspective.
Open the advisor review queue.
Search for the target review package.
Open the selected advisor review.
Enter advisor rationale.
Request evidence as a negative advisor outcome.
Return to the advisor review detail.
Enter revised advisor rationale.
Approve the package for compliance review.
Open the compliance queue.
Verify advisor approval did not create compliance release or client visibility.
journeys.proofClass: Human-speed advisor gate with internal draft boundary and no compliance release claim.

captions.intro: Advisor review can reject or approve internally, but it never becomes compliance release or client visibility.

journeys.name: Compliance blocker, evidence request, release and audit trail

valueNarrative.userNeed: Compliance needs to decide whether a recommendation is release-ready and make any blocker actionable.
valueNarrative.confidenceMoment: The decision room, evidence request and audit view show what was blocked, why, and what changed.
valueNarrative.safetyBoundary: Generic completion and unsafe release paths fail closed until controlled recovery and release conditions are met.
valueNarrative.resultValue: The released view is audit-backed and client-safe, not a vague acceptance or hidden bypass.

businessSteps: Authenticate as compliance.
Open the compliance queue.
Search for the Northbridge review package.
Open the selected decision room.
Attempt the unsafe generic completion through the API gate.
Observe the blocked compliance state.
Open the evidence request modal.
Enter the controlled evidence-request reason.
Acknowledge the no-release guard.
Submit the exact request-evidence phrase.
Return to the decision room.
Open the release/audit sibling view.
Verify audit trail entries are present.
Open the released decision success surface.
Confirm release is client-safe and audit-backed, not client acceptance.
journeys.proofClass: Compliance gate proof with blocked precondition, controlled recovery and audit-visible release boundary.

captions.intro: Compliance blocks unsafe release, requests recovery evidence, and records an audit-backed release boundary.

journeys.name: Client-safe projection, redaction, approval and download boundary

valueNarrative.userNeed: The client/export team needs to package only released, redacted information without turning preview into delivery.
valueNarrative.confidenceMoment: Projection, scope, redaction, preview, approval and download each remain visible as separate decisions.
valueNarrative.safetyBoundary: Wrong-role approval, unreleased draft content and preview-as-download all fail closed.
valueNarrative.resultValue: The final package can be generated only after released content, redaction and approval have all been proven.

businessSteps: Open the unreleased decision projection.
Verify internal draft text is absent.
Open the released decision success projection.
Verify released client-safe summary is present.
Switch to compliance export perspective.
Open export scope selection.
Select or verify released client-safe content.
Validate redaction through the export API.
Open the redaction review screen.
Open the approval preview.
Verify preview does not download or share.
Attempt approval from the wrong family-CFO perspective.
Observe the blocked approval proof.
Return to compliance approval/download flow.
Confirm download stays gated until approval and generation complete.
journeys.proofClass: Client-safe projection and export boundary proof with redaction, preview-not-download and approval-before-download evidence.

captions.intro: The export journey starts from released client-safe content and keeps preview, approval, download and sharing separate.
